# Hypercap Trial Adverse Events

Repository: https://github.com/reblocke/Hypercap-Trial-Adverse-Events
Primary scholarly record: CHEST 2024 abstract, "Hypercapnic respiratory failure is infrequently reported as an adverse event in clinical trials"
DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.chest.2024.06.3377
Citation: Anderson-Bell D, Locke BW. CHEST. 2024;166(4):A5688.

Purpose:
This public repository contains code and author-owned poster materials for an abstract analyzing how often hypercapnic respiratory failure, hypoventilation, respiratory depression, and related adverse-event terms are reported in ClinicalTrials.gov structured trial results.

Source data:
- Du J, Shi X ClinicalTrials.gov structured results dataset.
- Source paper: https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-023-02869-7
- Figshare collection: https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.c.6860254.v1
- Expected local files are data/raw/csv/efficacy_df.csv and data/raw/csv/safety_df.csv.
- Source data are public third-party data; link and cite the canonical records rather than committing mirrored archives.

Primary workflow:
```bash
stata-mp -b do ClinicalTrialsGov.do data/raw outputs/stata
```

Default behavior:
- If no arguments are supplied, ClinicalTrialsGov.do reads from data/raw and writes to outputs/stata.
- Generated Stata logs, derived .dta files, and Excel exports belong under outputs/stata.
- Root-level generated files such as by_arm_data.dta, by_trial_data.dta, trials_data.dta, "by arm data.xlsx", and "by trial data.xlsx" are legacy generated artifacts and should not be committed.

Repository layout:
- README.md: human-facing overview, run instructions, citation, and data boundaries.
- CITATION.cff: structured repository and CHEST abstract citation metadata.
- ClinicalTrialsGov.do: Stata workflow for ClinicalTrials.gov adverse-event aggregation.
- FAERS access.ipynb: optional exploratory OpenFDA/FAERS notebook; not the canonical abstract workflow.
- abstract/: author-owned CHEST poster PDF and Markdown summary.
- data_dictionary.md and data_dictionary.csv: variable/source/output dictionary.
- AGENTS.md: repository-specific rules for future automated edits.

Data and license boundaries:
- No PHI or private clinical data are expected in this repository.
- Do not add local data mirrors, OpenFDA caches, Stata derived datasets, generated Excel outputs, notebook outputs, or third-party reference PDFs to the public branch.
- Repository code and author-owned documentation are MIT licensed.
- External datasets, publisher pages, and third-party reference materials remain under their own terms.

Agent cautions:
- This is an abstract/poster repository, not a full manuscript repository.
- There is no PMCID for this CHEST abstract. Do not invent a PMCID or add unrelated PMCID records.
- Preserve legacy Stata variable spellings such as pos_hypoven_* unless a scientific refactor is explicitly requested; document them in the data dictionary.
- Keep the CHEST abstract DOI 10.1016/j.chest.2024.06.3377 as the primary scholarly citation.
- Keep the Du/Shi Scientific Data and Figshare records as source-data citations, not as the repository's primary scholarly output.
